answer:
\boxed{B}

The World Health Organization (WHO) defines anemia in pregnancy as a hemoglobin level of **less than 11.0 g/dL (gm%)** to account for the physiological dilution of blood volume during gestation. This threshold is lower than the general adult cutoff (12.0 g/dL) to ensure early detection and intervention. Option B (11.0 gm%) aligns with this guideline.
